steps.prompt: Two gates in a row — first an or gate, then an and gate. What do you think happens if you turn switch A on?
steps.feedback: First gate is on — but the lamp is still out! The signal has to travel through both gates. Turn switch C on too.
The lamp lights up! First the or gate, then the and gate — two steps to get there. Now turn C off — what happens?
Lamp goes out — the and gate needs C. The chain broke! First gate, then second gate, then lamp — all must work.
